note: PRE-SPLIT nominal price. Modern feeds split-adjust the whole history (AAPL 4:1 split on 2020-08-31, AFTER as_of -> factor 4), so the contemporaneous nominal close is recovered as Yahoo raw_close x 4. raw close today reads 22.585 x4 = 90.34 nominal, exactly the card. Independent corroboration that does NOT come from the price feed: Q2 FY2016 10-Q (S005, filed 2016-04-27) share count 5,478,446 thousand x card mcap $494.9B = $90.34/sh. Plus dated contemporaneous market data (S008 as-of quotes CSV, S009 money.com 2016-05-12) showing the first sub-$90 close since 2014. This is the pre-split-case pattern (same as the NVDA exemplar).

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Multi-year-low entry BY DESIGN: the case deliberately tests buying a durable quality_compounder mispriced as ex-growth after its first revenue decline in 13 years. The as-of close $90.34 sits +0.97% above the trailing-52wk intraday low $89.47 -- which was set ON the as-of day itself (first sub-$90 print since 2014, intraday low documented in the card). Hugging the 52wk low is the intended setup, not a low-grab error.

live_fields.value: Q3 FY2016 guidance (issued with the Q2 print): revenue $41-43B, gross margin 37.5-38.0% (down from 39.4% in Q2) -- the margin-compression guide that is part of the bear optic at as_of.

As of 2016-05-12: no single company-defining litigation event drives the thesis. Standard 10-Q risk factors only (S005). The China/Greater-China -26% YoY weakness and Icahn's late-Apr-2016 exit on China-policy risk (S011) are the live qualitative overhang, not a lawsuit.

sources.note: standard risk factors; no material new litigation flagged
